generated_oml:
// C2: Physical Acting Components
instance OccupancyControlActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Building Control System actuator for occupancy-based lighting/HVAC control, integrated with BIM via model-driven transformations for real-time adjustments"
]

instance HVACControlActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "HVAC system control actuator with adaptive temperature/humidity regulation enabled by reinforcement learning policies in BOREALIS"
]

instance LightingControlActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Adaptive lighting actuator with dynamic brightness control based on occupancy and energy efficiency optimization"
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance TemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "IoT-enabled environmental sensor deployed in building infrastructure for real-time temperature monitoring with metadata extracted from enriched BIM models"
    DTDFVocab:producedData TemperatureDataTransmitted
]

instance HumiditySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "IoT sensor measuring relative humidity levels with automated metadata extraction from BIM for bidirectional communication with DT"
    DTDFVocab:producedData HumidityDataTransmitted
]

instance OccupancySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Post-occupancy IoT sensor detecting building occupancy patterns for energy efficiency optimization and comfort analysis"
    DTDFVocab:producedData OccupancyDataTransmitted
]

instance EnergyConsumptionSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Real-time energy consumption tracker integrated with BIM metadata for DT analysis of operational efficiency"
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnergyDataTransmitted
]

instance CO2Sensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Indoor air quality sensor measuring CO₂ levels for ventilation optimization and indoor environmental comfort monitoring"
    DTDFVocab:producedData CO2DataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Data Transmission
instance TemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TemperatureSensor
]

instance HumidityDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HumiditySensor
]

instance OccupancyDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OccupancySensor
]

instance EnergyDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnergyConsumptionSensor
]

instance CO2DataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CO2Sensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance BuildingBIMModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "3D spatial and functional representation of the LEED Platinum academic building with embedded runtime semantics for sensor/actuator metadata and control logic via model-driven transformations"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ModelTransformationEngine
]

instance OperationalDataStore : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Structured storage of real-time operational data extracted from IoT sensors and BCS via Eclipse Hono/Ditto middleware for analytics"
    DTDFVocab:fromData TemperatureDataTransmitted, HumidityDataTransmitted,
                          OccupancyDataTransmitted, EnergyDataTransmitted, CO2DataTransmitted
]

instance VisualizationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Interactive 3D Unreal Engine 5 model derived from BIM that dynamically updates with DT data for immersive facility management visualization"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo UnrealEngine5
]

// C11: Enablers
instance BIMEnrichmentTool : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Model-driven engineering tool that enriches BIM models with device/sensor metadata and control logic for automated runtime interface generation"
]

instance EclipseHonoMiddleware : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Real-time IoT middleware facilitating bidirectional communication between physical sensors/actuators and DT via endpoint addresses and device IDs extracted from BIM"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DittoPlatform
]

instance DittoPlatform : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Open-source Digital Twin platform handling data synchronization and service orchestration for runtime artifact generation from BIM models"
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictiveAnalyticsService
    DTDFVocab:inputTo StandardizedExtensionPoints
]

instance UnrealEngine5 : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Rich visualization environment providing interactive 3D monitoring and simulation capabilities for DT data visualization"
]

instance ModelTransformationEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Automated model-to-model/model-to-text transformation system generating runtime interfaces (Hono/Ditto configurations) from enriched BIM models"
]

instance StandardizedExtensionPoints : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Modular architecture framework enabling custom service integration (e.g., AI analytics, IoT plugins) without redeploying core DT infrastructure"
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing real-time monitoring and visualization of building performance metrics (energy consumption, occupancy) via Unreal Engine 5 visualizations"
    DTDFVocab:provides BuildingPerformanceInsight
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationModel
]

instance PredictiveAnalyticsService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Optimization service using reinforcement learning (BOREALIS) to generate actionable recommendations for energy efficiency, comfort, and cost savings"
    DTDFVocab:provides EnergyOptimizationRecommendation
    DTDFVocab:enables EclipseHonoMiddleware
]

instance PostOccupancyAnalyticsService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Iterative refinement service adjusting control policies (adaptive lighting/HVAC) based on live sensor data for improved operational outcomes"
    DTDFVocab:provides PolicyRefinementAction
    DTDFVocab:enables ModelTransformationEngine
]

instance DecisionAutomationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Virtual-to-physical interaction service automating adjustments to Building Control Systems (BCS) via control signals for reduced manual intervention"
    DTDFVocab:provides ControlSignalAction
    DTDFVocab:enables StandardizedExtensionPoints
]

instance CustomServiceIntegrationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Modular service enabling deployment of domain-specific plugins (IoT analytics, AI decision support) through standardized extension points"
    DTDFVocab:provides CustomPluginAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance BuildingPerformanceInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Data-driven assessment of building performance metrics (energy consumption, occupancy patterns, indoor environmental quality) visualized in real-time via Unreal Engine 5"
]

instance EnergyOptimizationRecommendation : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predictive analytics output recommending energy efficiency measures (adaptive HVAC, lighting optimization) based on reinforcement learning models"
]

instance PolicyRefinementAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated adjustment of control policies (adaptive lighting/HVAC) through iterative refinement based on live sensor data for improved operational efficiency"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ControlSignalAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Virtual-to-physical control signal generation for BCS components (lighting, HVAC) to automate operational adjustments based on DT analysis"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance CustomPluginAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Deployment of domain-specific services (IoT analytics, AI decision support) through standardized extension points without redeploying core DT infrastructure"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// C12: Environment
instance BuildingEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains OccupancyControlActuator, HVACControlActuator, LightingControlActuator,
                  TemperatureSensor, HumiditySensor, OccupancySensor,
                  EnergyConsumptionSensor, CO2Sensor
]

instance OperationalEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains BuildingBIMModel, OperationalDataStore,
          VisualizationModel, EclipseHonoMiddleware,
          DittoPlatform, ModelTransformationEngine
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "Control signals and commands generated by DT services are transmitted back to physical building systems via **Eclipse Hono/Ditto middleware**, enabling automated adjustments to BCS (e.g., dynamic lighting control, HVAC optimization). The framework leverages **BIM-based control logic modeling** to define actuator configurations and control strategies, ensuring seamless integration with physical infrastructure. This interaction supports **adaptive lighting systems** (e.g., BOREALIS project) and **energy-efficient operations** through reinforcement learning-driven policies."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The DT operates on a **real-time to near-real-time** time scale, with synchronization rates determined by sensor data acquisition frequencies. The framework supports:
  - **Low-latency bidirectional communication** via **Eclipse Hono/Ditto**, ensuring sub-second updates for critical control signals.
  - **Iterative refinement** of control policies (e.g., BOREALIS’s reinforcement learning) over time, aligning DT behavior with evolving operational data.
  - **Event-driven processing** for asynchronous updates (e.g., occupancy changes, energy spikes), enabling adaptive DT responses."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The DT constellation is a **service-oriented, layered architecture** with the following orchestration:
  - **Core DT layer**: Centralized runtime interface (e.g., Hono/Ditto) managing bidirectional data exchange with physical assets.
  - **BIM integration layer**: Enriched BIM models serve as the semantic foundation for device modeling and control logic definition.
  - **Visualization layer**: Unreal Engine 5 provides interactive 3D representations of the DT, synchronized with real-time data.
  - **Service layer**: Extensible extension points allow DT Engineers to deploy domain-specific plugins (e.g., energy optimization, facility management dashboards).
  - **Control layer**: Real-time synchronization with physical BCS via Hono/Ditto, enabling adaptive operations (e.g., adaptive lighting/HVAC via reinforcement learning)."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The DT spans all life cycle stages of the building:
  1. **Design phase**: Enriched BIM models include static and dynamic BCS components (e.g., sensor/actuator placements, control logic) for automated DT generation.
  2. **Construction phase**: BIM models are validated and transformed into runtime artifacts (e.g., Hono/Ditto configurations) for initial DT deployment.
  3. **Operation phase**: Real-time synchronization with physical assets via **Eclipse Hono/Ditto**, enabling adaptive control (e.g., BOREALIS’s reinforcement learning) and continuous optimization.
  4. **Refurbishment phase**: DTs support post-occupancy data analysis to inform retrofitting decisions, with modular extensions allowing incremental upgrades."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The DT fidelity is evaluated using the **Overall Digital Twin Entanglement (ODTE) metric**, which measures the alignment between the DT and its physical counterpart. Key considerations include:
  - **Semantic coherence**: Automated extraction of BIM metadata ensures consistent representation of physical assets (e.g., sensor placements, control logic).
  - **Real-time synchronization**: Eclipse Hono/Ditto middleware minimizes latency in data exchange, reducing semantic drift (as highlighted in Abbasi et al.’s work).
  - **Validation mechanisms**: Model validation steps (e.g., IFC compliance checks) and iterative refinement (e.g., BOREALIS’s reinforcement learning) enhance DT validity.
  - **Uncertainty management**: While not explicitly quantified, the framework assumes that BIM models serve as the primary source of truth, with runtime data (e.g., sensor noise) handled via middleware protocols."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The DT technical connection relies on:
  1. **Network protocols**: **Eclipse Hono** and **Ditto** use lightweight, real-time protocols (e.g., MQTT, WebSockets) for bidirectional communication between physical sensors/actuators and DT services.
  2. **Middleware architecture**: A layered system coupling BIM models (static) with runtime interfaces (e.g., Hono/Ditto configurations), enabling seamless data flow.
  3. **Standardized APIs**: Extension points allow integration with third-party services (e.g., IoT platforms, AI analytics) via RESTful or gRPC interfaces.
  4. **Latency optimization**: Designed for sub-second response times to support real-time control (e.g., adaptive lighting/HVAC)."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The DT is hosted and deployed via:
  1. **Cloud/edge deployment**: Middleware (Hono/Ditto) can run on edge devices (e.g., IoT gateways) or cloud-based platforms for scalability.
  2. **Unreal Engine 5**: Visualization layer deployed as a standalone application or integrated into the DT orchestration stack.
  3. **Standardized extension points**: Allow modular deployment of custom services (e.g., AI models, IoT plugins) without altering core DT infrastructure.
  4. **BIM-centric workflows**: Deployment begins with BIM model enrichment and automated transformation into runtime artifacts (e.g., Hono/Ditto configurations), reducing manual effort."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The DT supports **horizontal integration** with external systems via:
  1. **Standardized APIs**: Extension points enable integration with third-party IoT platforms, BIM tools (e.g., IFC), and analytics services (e.g., AI-driven decision support).
  2. **BIM interoperability**: Enriched BIM models serve as a unified semantic foundation for collaboration across AECO stakeholders (e.g., architects, engineers, facility managers).
  3. **Middleware bridges**: Eclipse Hono/Ditto facilitate data exchange with legacy systems or proprietary IoT platforms, ensuring compatibility with heterogeneous infrastructures."
]

instance data_ownership_and_privacy : DTDFVocab:DataOwnershipPrivacy [
    base:desc "Data ownership and privacy are managed through:
  1. **BIM-centric governance**: Building control engineers define ownership of BIM-enriched data (e.g., sensor metadata, control logic) during deployment.
  2. **Middleware security**: Eclipse Hono/Ditto support encryption (e.g., TLS) and access controls to protect real-time data exchange.
  3. **Compliance frameworks**: Aligns with AECO standards (e.g., IFC) and regulatory mandates (e.g., GDPR) for data privacy.
  4. **Non-functional requirements**: Ensure low-overhead updates and scalable deployment without compromising security."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The framework adheres to the following standards and methodologies:
  1. **Building Information Modeling (BIM)**: IFC (Industry Foundation Classes) format for semantic interoperability.
  2. **Digital Twin standards**: Eclipse Ditto and Eclipse Hono follow open-source IoT and DT protocols (e.g., MQTT, WebSockets).
  3. **Model-driven engineering (MDE)**: Automated transformations (model-to-model/model-to-text) for DT generation.
  4. **AECO-specific standards**: Integration with BIM tools (e.g., Revit, ArchiCAD) and compliance with industry practices (e.g., LEED certification for the Living Lab case study)."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "Security and safety are addressed through:
  1. **Encrypted communication**: Eclipse Hono/Ditto middleware uses TLS/SSL for secure data transmission between physical sensors and DT services.
  2. **Access controls**: Standardized extension points enforce role-based permissions (e.g., Building Control Engineers, Facility Managers) to prevent unauthorized access.
  3. **Fault tolerance**: Real-time synchronization via Hono/Ditto ensures resilience to network failures or sensor malfunctions.
  4. **Human safety**: Virtual-to-physical interactions (e.g., control signals) are designed to avoid accidental harm to occupants, with validation steps ensuring compliance with operational safety protocols."
]
